sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Levante UD are in poor form, having lost their last three matches and conceding regularly with no clean sheet in seven games. Athletic Club, despite a mixed run with two losses in their last five, showed resilience with a recent win and a draw, though their away form remains inconsistent.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Athletic Club have the upper hand, with a 3-1 victory in their most recent clash in March 2022 and a goalless draw prior to that. Athletic remain unbeaten in their last nine matches against Levante, suggesting a psychological edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Levante UD, sitting 19th with 9 points, struggle defensively, conceding 24 goals (19th in the league), while their attack has managed 16 goals. Athletic Club, 8th with 17 points, have a more balanced setup but lack firepower, scoring just 12 goals this season.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Levante, Etta Eyong (6 goals) and Iván Romero (4 goals) are crucial in attack, while Athletic's Álex Berenguer, despite no goals this season, has a history of impacting games against Levante with a high average rating in past matchups.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Levante's low possession (39%) and high clearance rate (30.15 per game) indicate a defensive, counter-attacking style, while Athletic's ability to score late (35% of goals after the 76th minute) could test Levante's endurance in the closing stages.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Levante's struggles and Athletic's historical dominance in this fixture, the away side might have a slight edge despite Sofascore's 42% home win prediction. A tight, low-scoring game is likely based on head-to-head trends of under 2.5 goals in recent meetings.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Athletic Club leads Levante UD 2-0 in a match that has seen the away side dominate key moments. Athletic Club struck early with a goal in the 3rd minute from Robert Navarro, setting the tone for the first half, and added a second just before the break through Nico Williams in the 44th minute. Levante UD, despite having slightly more ball possession, has struggled to convert their control into meaningful chances, failing to create any big opportunities. Athletic Club, on the other hand, has been clinical, capitalizing on their attacking entries and creating multiple big chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half largely favored Athletic Club, especially in the opening minutes where they established a strong attacking presence and sustained pressure leading to their first goal. Levante UD showed brief spells of attacking intent around the middle of the half, pushing forward with intent but failing to break through. Athletic Club regained control towards the latter stages, mounting consistent pressure that culminated in their second goal. Levante UD had a late surge before the whistle, but it wasn’t enough to shift the tide.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance breakdown
halftimeSections.text: Athletic Club’s first-half performance was defined by efficiency in the final third, creating multiple big chances and converting two of them. Their players like Nico Williams and Álex Berenguer have been pivotal, with the latter assisting both goals. Defensively, they’ve been solid, winning a decent share of duels and limiting Levante’s penetration into dangerous areas. Levante UD, despite holding more possession, lacked creativity and sharpness in attack, with no big chances created and minimal threat in the penalty area. Their defense has also been vulnerable, conceding space to Athletic’s quick forwards.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Athletic Club, Nico Williams stands out with his goal and constant threat on the wing, while Álex Berenguer’s playmaking ability has been crucial in setting up scoring opportunities. Robert Navarro also made his mark with an early goal. On Levante’s side, players like Etta Eyong and Goduine Koyalipou showed some intent with shots on target, but they need more support from midfield to turn their efforts into goals. Carlos Álvarez could be key for Levante in the second half if he can capitalize on his creative passing.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD will need to come out with a renewed sense of urgency in the second half if they hope to overturn the deficit. They must improve their attacking output and take risks to create chances, potentially exposing their defense further. Athletic Club, with a comfortable lead, might focus on managing the game, looking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities while maintaining a compact defensive shape. Their ability to strike quickly could be decisive if Levante pushes forward aggressively.
